name: Flood
description: Flash flood in the Greater Toronto Area. Triggered by extreme rainfall on the Don and Humber watersheds. Escalates over 13 minutes from water level warnings to multi-district emergency response.

don_flood_rescue.summary: Don Valley flooding — road closed, fire rescue deployed and on scene.
don_flood_rescue.main_event: Don Valley Pkwy flooded. Water rescue underway. 2 vehicles stranded.
don_flood_rescue.prediction: DVP closure likely 4–6 hours. Expect significant downtown traffic impact.
don_flood_rescue.suggestion: Close DVP southbound at Lawrence. Activate alternate route signage on Bayview and Leslie.

humber_flood.summary: Humber River overflowing banks near Old Mill. Evacuation underway.
humber_flood.main_event: Humber River overflowing. Riverside Drive flooded. Evacuation order issued.
humber_flood.prediction: Flooding may spread to Etobicoke Creek corridor if rainfall continues.
humber_flood.suggestion: Dispatch second water rescue team. Open Etobicoke Olympium as evacuation shelter.

flood_medical_rosedale.summary: Ambulance calls spiking — flood-related medical emergencies in Rosedale.
flood_medical_rosedale.main_event: Multiple flood-related medical calls in Rosedale and Cabbagetown.
flood_medical_rosedale.suggestion: Stage additional ambulances at Bloor and Sherbourne intersection.

lakeshore_inundation.summary: Lake Shore Blvd heavily flooded — vehicles submerged, civilian rescue needed.
lakeshore_inundation.main_event: Lake Shore Blvd near Bathurst impassable. Multiple vehicles submerged.
lakeshore_inundation.prediction: Water will continue rising for 20–30 minutes before rainfall upstream subsides.
lakeshore_inundation.suggestion: Deploy all available water rescue units to Lake Shore corridor.

predefined_analysis_result.description: Downtown Toronto baseline. Overcast skies, light rain beginning.
Don River water level sensor at Bloor St — rising 12 cm above seasonal average. Rain rate 42 mm/hr upstream. Early watch issued.
Humber River gauge near Weston — level rising rapidly. 18 cm above flood watch threshold. Second watershed affected.
Lower Don Trail underpass at Eastern Ave — flooded. Road impassable, 40 cm standing water.
Fire Department water rescue sirens heading east on Lake Shore Blvd toward Don Valley. Multiple units.
TFRS water rescue boats deployed on flooded Don Valley Pkwy southbound. Two vehicles stranded in approximately 1 metre of water.
DVP southbound fully closed at Richmond St E. Significant standing water, abandoned vehicles visible.
Don River now 78 cm above flood warning threshold and still rising. Flood Emergency declared for lower Don watershed.
Multiple ambulance sirens converging north of downtown. Flood-related medical calls increasing.
Paramedics responding to stranded resident in flooded basement — Rosedale Ave near Bloor.
Ham radio report: 'King St East completely flooded between Parliament and Sherbourne. Do not drive through. Water at door handles.'
Humber River overflowing banks near Old Mill. Riverside Drive flooded for 600 metres. Evacuation order for adjacent streets.
Second water rescue team dispatched to Humber River. At least 3 units confirmed from radio traffic.
Multiple residents evacuating on foot along Roncesvalles Ave heading north. Some carrying bags. Panic visible.
Citizen phone report: 'Lakeshore Blvd under 80cm of water near Bathurst. Cars floating. People stuck on car roofs. Need boats NOW.'
Lake Shore Blvd West near Bathurst — major inundation. Several vehicles partially submerged. Waterline at traffic light bases.
Ham radio net: Ryerson gym, Moss Park Arena, and Etobicoke Olympium confirmed as flood shelters. Operators requesting volunteers.
Don River water level beginning to recede — 5 cm drop in 20 minutes. Rain stopped upstream. Situation stabilizing.
Don Valley rescue winding down. Last stranded vehicle reached. 7 civilians rescued. 2 transported to hospital — hypothermia, non-life-threatening.
Flood event subsiding. DVP remains closed. Emergency services still active. Recovery phase beginning.
